title: System and method for storing energy
abstract: Disclosed is a method and system that compresses ionized gas is stored in at least one containment cell. The compressed ionized gas is transferred from the at least on containment cell to an expansion chamber in which a piston is situated; The piston moves based in part on a reaction between ionized gas in the expansion chamber and an electrical charge of a surface of a head of the piston. The movement of the piston drives a drive shaft. Movement of the drive shaft is converted to electrical energy.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS OF THE INVENTION What is claimed is: 1 . A method comprising: storing compressed ionized gas in at least one containment cell; transferring at least a portion of the compressed ionized gas from the at least on containment ceil to an expansion chamber in which a piston is situated; and moving the piston based in part on a reaction between ionized gas in the expansion chamber and an electrical charge of a surface of a head of the piston. 2. The method as in claim 1 , wherein moving the piston based in part on a reaction between ionized gas in the expansion chamber and an electrical charge of a surface of a head of the piston comprises: causing movement of a drive shaft in response to the moving of the piston; and capturing electrical energy produced as a result of the movement of the drive shaft operativeiy connected to a generator. 3. The method as in claim 1 , wherein storing compressed ionized gas in at least one containment cell comprises: injecting a first amount of compressed ionized gas into an inner cavity of a first containment cell; and injecting a second amount of compressed ionized gas into an inner cavity of a second containment cell, the first and second containment cells each comprising a corkscrew shaped tube. 4. The method as in claim 1 , wherein storing compressed ionized gas in at least one containment cell comprises: injecting an amount of a first compressed ionized gas into an inner cavity of a first containment ceil, the first compressed ionized gas comprising a positive charge polarity; and injecting an amount of a second compressed ionized gas into an inner cavity of a second containment ceil, the second compressed ionized gas comprising a negative 

 charge polarity, the first and second containment cells each comprising a corkscrew shaped tube. 5. The method as in claim 1 , wherein the at least one containment ceil comprises: a construction material with a dielectric strength equal to or greater than a threshold dielectric strength of 0.1 MV/m (0.1 x 10 6 Volt/meter). 8. The method as in claim 1 , wherein the at least one containment ceil comprises: a vacuum level meeting or exceeding a threshold vacuum level substantially at 10 ~2 Pascals. 7. The method as in claim 1 , further comprising: upon moving the piston, transferring the ionized gas from the expansion chamber to a deionization chamber; and converting the ionized gas, via precipitation, to a solid salt via a reaction between the ionized gas and a reactant in the deionization chamber, 8. A system comprising: at least one containment ceil for storing compressed ionized gas in at least one containment cell; an expansion chamber to receive at least a portion of the compressed ionized gas from the at least on containment cell; and a piston, situated within the expansion chamber, for movement based in part on a reaction between ionized gas in the expansion chamber and an electrical charge of a surface of a head of the piston. 9. The system as in claim 8, further comprising: a drive shaft that moves in response to the movement of the piston; and a generator, operatively connected to the drive shaft, to produce electrical energy from movement of the drive shaft. 10. The system as in claim 8, wherein the at least one containment ceil comprises: an inner cavity of a first containment cell into which a first amount of compressed ionized gas is injected; and an inner cavity of a second containment cell into which a second amount of the compressed ionized gas is injected;, the first and second containment ceils each comprising a corkscrew shaped tube. 1 1 . The system as in claim 8, wherein the at least one containment ceil comprises: an inner cavity of a first containment cell into which an amount of a first compressed ionized gas is injected, the first compressed ionized gas comprising a positive charge polarity; and an inner cavity of a second containment cell into which an amount of a second compressed ionized gas is injected, the second compressed ionized gas comprising a negative charge polarity, the first and second containment cells each comprising a corkscrew shaped tube. 12. The system as in claim 8, wherein the at least one containment ceil comprises: a construction material with a dielectric strength equal to or greater than a threshold dielectric strength of 0.1 MV/m (0.1 x 10 6 Volt/meter). 13. The system as in claim 8, wherein the at least one containment ceil comprises: a vacuum level meeting or exceeding a threshold vacuum level substantially at 10 ~2 Pascals. 14. The system as in claim 8, further comprising: a deionization chamber for receiving the ionized gas from the expansion chamber, the deionization chamber comprising a reactant that converts the ionized gas, via precipitation, to a solid salt via a reaction between the ionized gas and the reactant.
